The announcement comes one day after Swalwell suspended his campaign for California governor after the emergence of allegations that he continues to deny. Democrats quickly abandoned […]

title: VA Announces New Assistant Secretary for Operations, Security, and Preparedness | Reginald Neal
summary:
The Department of Veterans Affairs has named Reginald Neal as Assistant Secretary for the Office of Operations, Security, and Preparedness (OSP).

Bringing experience in battlefield leadership, federal service, and emergency management, Mr. Neal will help guide how VA prepares, protects, and responds nationwide in support of Veterans.

His leadership reflects a focus on readiness, coordination, and ensuring VA can operate effectively when it matters most.

summary: After falling sharply during the pandemic, California’s prison population now stands at its lowest point in more than 30 years. Meanwhile, the average annual cost of imprisoning a person has risen 40% since 2019.

title: PTSD Treatment Decision Aid AD
summary: There are several effective PTSD treatments. This means that if you have PTSD, you have options. The PTSD Treatment Decision Aid helps you learn about these options and think about which treatment might be best for you. It also gives you tips on how to work with your provider to decide on a treatment.

title: Hiring Veterans
summary:
If you are a Veteran looking for work, Vet Resources can help. Hiring Veterans regularly highlights job openings from employers across the country looking to hire Veterans. 
Many listings close quickly, so visit News.VA.gov today and search hiring Veterans. Veterans bring skill, discipline, leadership, and problem solving that make organizations stronger. 
Our adaptability, perspective, and work ethic help drive innovation and strength in workplace culture.

— 
 
Explore VA.gov to learn about your benefits. 
 
Print out your VA Welcome Kit: https://www.va.gov/welcome-kit/ 
 
Whether you’re just getting out of the service or you’ve been a civilian for years now, the VA Welcome Kit can help guide you to the benefits and services you’ve earned. Based on where you are in life, your VA benefits and services can support you in different ways. 
 
Sign up for the #VetResources email newsletter: https://news.va.gov/vetresources/ 
 
#VetResources features Veteran-centric news stories, various deals and discounts, employment opportunities, and much more. 
 
#Veterans #VA #VABenefits #Healthcare

title: 40th Annual National Disabled Veterans Winter Sports Clinic
summary:
That is a wrap on the 40th Annual National Disabled Veterans Winter Sports Clinic. Enjoy highlights from this historic adaptive sports event, held annually in Snowmass, Colorado. 

Learn more: www.wintersportsclinic.org

U.S. Department of Veterans Affairs: www.va.gov

Proudly supported by DAV: www.dav.org

#VeteransFirst #WinterSportsClinic #AdaptiveSports #DisabledVeterans #DAV #recreationtherapy #asap #armedservicesartspartnership #sports4vets

title: CIA Insider: Why the Iran Ceasefire is Extremely Dangerous
summary:
Retired CIA Operations Officer Sam Faddis breaks down the fragile new ceasefire with Iran and why the negotiated terms should worry every American.

In this hard-hitting episode, Sam reveals:
• Why the current Iran ceasefire is far more dangerous than it looks
• Iran’s true intentions driven by radical religious beliefs
• The hidden nuclear weapons strategy that changes everything
• How civilian intelligence and military operators actually work together
• The incredible story of American airmen recovered from a hostile environment

From fragile diplomatic deals to high-stakes nuclear brinkmanship and real-world rescue operations — this is unfiltered insight from a career CIA veteran who has seen it all.

Timestamps:
00:00 Ceasefire with Iran
11:00 Nuclear Weapons and Strategy
17:29 Recovery of Airmen

Guest: Retired CIA Operations Officer Sam Faddis

summary: Nearly three in four Californians enroll in college right after high school. But whether—and where—high school graduates attend college varies across regions and demographic groups.

title: College Readiness in California
summary: More Californians are graduating from high school, but many graduates are underprepared for higher education, and college readiness varies by race/ethnicity and income levels. Preparing students for college requires coordination between K–12 and higher education systems.

source: The Official United States Marine Corps Public Website News
title: Motor Transport Unveils Integration Lab, Driving Tactical Wheeled Vehicle Modernization
summary: Previously, Marine Corps program offices integrating new systems onto the Joint Light Tactical Vehicle faced significant logistical challenges. The process involved requesting a vehicle from Program Executive Officer Land Systems’ (PEO LS) Program Manager Motor Transport (PM Motor-T), costly and complex vehicle shipments, and potential roadblocks to project deadlines because of vehicle availability constraints. This system created inefficiencies and drove up costs.

title: Has Universal Access to Transfer-Level Courses Changed Student Outcomes at California Community Colleges?
summary: Since the California Community College system began to overhaul its remediation policies in 2019, completion of transfer-level math courses has increased; transfer rates to four-year colleges have also risen. However, gains have varied across demographic groups, and many students who do not complete transfer-level math on the first try leave the system without transferring or earning degrees.

title: Unmasking Terrorism: The Truth Behind Recent Attacks
summary:

It emphasizes the need for accurate reporting, effective law enforcement, and personal vigilance in the current security landscape.

Takeaways

  • Law Enforcement Response
  • Individual Preparedness

title: From San Bernardino to Foiled SF Plots: The Real Work of the Joint Terrorism Task Force
summary:
In this eye-opening episode of Your LEO Nation, hosted by retired California Highway Patrol Chief Mark Garrett, we sit down with special guest Nelson Duarte for an in-depth discussion on the Joint Terrorism Task Force (JTTF)—its structure, nationwide operations, and critical role in safeguarding communities.

Nelson Duarte shares firsthand insights from his experience, particularly highlighting the California Highway Patrol's (CHP) unique contributions to JTTF efforts in preventing and investigating terrorist threats. The conversation uncovers the often-unseen work of dedicated law enforcement professionals who serve as the first line of defense against terrorism.

Key highlights include:

  • Detailed breakdown of JTTF organization and collaboration across federal, state, and local agencies
  • CHP's respected position and integration within JTTF teams
  • In-depth look at the 2015 San Bernardino terrorist attack and how JTTF partners responded and investigated
  • The Everitt Aaron Jameson case (often referred to as Everett Jamerson) in San Francisco—a foiled ISIS-inspired plot targeting Pier 39 on Christmas Day
  • The vital, behind-the-scenes efforts of unsung heroes who thwart attacks before they happen
  • Ongoing terrorism threats, ideological battles, leadership's influence on law enforcement morale and effectiveness, the open border crisis, and the broader discussion around perceived threats like white supremacy

This episode shines a light on the real-world challenges, successes, and sacrifices of those protecting our nation—often without the recognition they deserve. Gain a deeper appreciation for the tireless work that keeps us safe and why public support and awareness matter more than ever.

Timestamps / Chapters:

  • 00:00 Introduction to JTTF and Guest Introduction
  • 05:19 JTTF Structure in California and Across the Country
  • 10:49 Role of CHP in JTTF and Perception Among Other Agencies
  • 17:01 San Bernardino Terrorist Attack and JTTF's Role
  • 27:26 Case of Everett Jamerson in San Francisco
  • 32:51 Preventing Terrorist Attacks
  • 38:20 Prevention and Ongoing Threats
  • 44:38 Impact of Leadership on Law Enforcement
  • 50:52 Open Border Crisis

If you're passionate about law enforcement, national security, or simply want to hear authentic stories from the front lines, this is a must-watch. Like, comment, subscribe, and hit the bell for more episodes of Your LEO Nation—where we honor the men and women who serve and protect.

#LawEnforcement #JTTF #TerrorismPrevention #CaliforniaHighwayPatrol #SanBernardino #PublicSafety #YourLEONation #MarkGarrett #NelsonDuarte
